claims.claim: Excited about stablecoins & onchain payments as crypto's clearest product-market fit, crossing into real payments/remittances/treasury
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about AI × crypto as complementary — crypto as payment/coordination layer for autonomous agents, verifiable provenance, decentralized compute/data markets
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about scaling infrastructure (ZK proofs, rollups, account abstraction) as maturing plumbing
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Excited about DePIN — token incentives bootstrapping real-world physical networks (compute/storage/bandwidth/energy)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about memecoins / pure speculation as negative-sum and reputationally damaging
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about mercenary-yield DeFi / circular leverage (emissions/points-farming TVL with no real economic activity)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concerned about ecosystem fragmentation (L2/app-chain proliferation fragmenting liquidity/users; interop must catch up)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Risk regime neutral tactically but structurally constructive multi-year (regulatory clarity, maturing infra, AI tailwind); thinks in adoption-decades, not market regimes
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Strong new stance that AI is a net positive for cybersecurity — defenders should aggressively use AI to red-team and find bugs ('arm the defenders'); a major theme not anticipated by the AI×crypto thesis
Repeated anti-AI-doomer stance, explicitly criticizing Anthropic's 'doomsday marketing' and calling AI-fear 'completely backwards'
Active tokenomics view: advocated buy-and-burn plus 'print-and-spend'/spend-to-grow and 'revenue is good' — specific token-design opinions the prediction (empty token arrays) didn't anticipate

Excitement about onchain capital markets — perpetual futures for price discovery, prediction markets, onchain lending/credit, and tokenization of traditional assets/RWAs — a sector cluster absent from the prediction
DeFi-security framing that recent hacks stemmed from inadvertent centralization and 'more decentralization, not less' would have mitigated them
Enthusiasm for AI-assisted/'vibe' coding and automated build loops
notes: Most claims are adjudicated almost entirely from the co-authored 2026-05-05 Crypto Fund 5 essay and the 2026-05-13 security podcast. Stablecoins, AI×crypto agent/coordination, and the structurally-constructive long-horizon risk stance are confirmed near-verbatim by the fund essay; memecoin concern is confirmed by the 2026-05-02 'memecoins were damaging to crypto... things have changed' reaffirmation. Scaling-infra (ZK/rollups/AA) is absent — the essay praises fast/cheap settlement as an outcome but never names the scaling tech. Mercenary-yield/circular-leverage concern is absent; his value-capture/'revenue is good' tweets are thematically adjacent (favoring real economics over subsidy-dependence) but voice a positive prescription rather than the predicted concern. Fragmentation/interop is absent entirely. DePIN is a weak confirm — only a passing positive listing of 'decentralized infrastructure' as a real-product area in the co-authored essay, without the token-incentive thesis. No contradictions. Much of the window's volume is AI-general (coding, doom debate, security), reflecting a heavier AI-as-defense emphasis than the prediction's crypto-centric AI×crypto framing.
